boat.construction_details: Mahogany marine ply with additional oak ribs, copper riveted oak ribs, solid mahogany seat and transom,

for_sales.sales_text: Please see the boat's description.  Note it comes with two rigs.

boat.full_description:
Nutz, formerly named Elsie Fay is a classic 12ft clinker built dinghy built in2003 with marine ply with epoxy resin by a carpenter Richard Herbert in Essex and with the class name Oboe. Number eight in a series of thirty. It conforms to a traditional clinker one design as found on the East Coast of Britain.

Makers specification The dinghy is handcrafted in mahogany marine plywood, solid mahogany and oak. Construction is glued clinker planking with copper riveted oak ribs based upon traditional designs by Howard Chappelle. Spars are clear fir, lines, sheets and halyards in white cotton-effect polyester and the dinghy is finished in clear yacht varnish to bring out the beauty of the wood. The boat is part of a limited edition and each has a signed certificate.

Nutz offers the choice of sailing with either one of two different rigs. A simple balanced Lug tan sail where the mast is stepped up front and secured to the forward transom. This is good for single handed sailing and is quick to rig. The other rig supplied is a Gunter rig with the mast in a stayed position sat on the centre transom. This has a mainsail hoisted on a gaff spar and a jib. This is good for a crew and for a more spirited sail. Both rigs share the boom and mast.

Sails are by Jeckell of Wroxham.

Hull in marine ply laid in a lapstrake or clinker format, sealed with epoxy resin. Hull has additional timber frames and copper roving.

Seat and rear transom in solid mahogany. Centreboard, tiller, rudder and oars in mixed solid wood and ply.

All fittings in Brass, including folding rowlocks.

Marine quality buoyancy bags.

The boat was designed by the builder to be up to Class C inshore coastal waters standard.

Previously used at sea around Mersea Island, more recently inland lake use in Surrey.
boat.name: Nutz
boat.short_description: Traditional clinker built sailing dinghy with the benefit of two rigs. Gunter, where mast is stayed and Lug where the mast is stepped forward and is unstayed.
